# The Hidden Costs of Overpricing

> 20 Ways Sellers Lose Money Without Knowing It. A seller's guide by Patti Smith, independent broker of Patti Smith Real Estate in Georgetown, California, serving El Dorado County since 1992 (CA DRE #01110483).

## About the book
The Hidden Costs of Overpricing names twenty specific, often invisible ways an overpriced listing quietly costs a seller money, and shows how to price an El Dorado County home right from day one.

## The 20 costs
- The Day One Freshness Premium (Why I Never Let My Sellers Waste Their Best Shot)
- Lost in the Filters (How Overpricing Makes Your Home Invisible to the Right Buyers)
- The Stain of Time (How Days on Market Weaken Your Story)
- When the Appraisal Turns Against You (Why Overpricing Makes Valuation Your Problem to Solve)
- The Low Offer Spiral (How Overpricing Invites Anchors and Hardball Tactics)
- When Enthusiasm Dies (How Overpricing Turns Agents and Buyers Away)
- Selling the Competition (How Overpricing Makes Other Homes Look Like Better Value)
- The Price Reduction Trap (Why Cutting Later Signals Weakness Instead of Strength)
- The Silent Drain of Carrying Costs (How Waiting Eats Away at Your Net Without You Noticing)
- The Net Shrinks (Why Overpricing Leaves You With Less Than If You Had Priced Right)
- Chasing the Market Down (How Waiting Turns Small Cuts Into Bigger Losses)
- The Leverage Flip (How Overpricing Shifts Power From You to the Buyer)
- The Buyer's Suspicion Loop (How Lingering on the Market Creates Doubt That Grows)
- The Open House Silence (How Thin Traffic Creates Doubt and Weakens Your Sale)
- The Stigma of Price History (How Every Cut Leaves a Trail Buyers Use Against You)
- The Missed Window of Opportunity (How Overpricing Costs You the Best Season to Sell)
- The Double Mortgage Stress (How Overpricing Forces You to Carry Two Homes at Once)
- The Emotional Erosion (How Overpricing Wears You Down Before You Even Close)
- The Risk of a Broken Escrow (How Overpricing Raises the Odds of a Deal Falling Apart)
- The Opportunity Cost (How Overpricing Steals Your Future as Well as Your Present)
